stories.title: Blue Jays 7-2 Over Braves — Chad Dallas Debuts, Sale Falls to 8-4

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Chad Dallas made his MLB debut Thursday.

He combined with five Toronto pitchers to hold the best team in baseball to 4 hits.

Blue Jays 7, Braves 2. Chris Sale falls to 8-4.

#BlueJays #Braves #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Chris Sale allowed 10 hits in 5.2 innings Thursday night. Record drops to 8-4.

The Braves are still 42-20 and lead the NL East by 9.5 games.

Even the best teams have bad nights.

#Braves #MLB

facebook.long_form:
The best team in baseball had a rough night on Thursday. The Toronto Blue Jays beat the Atlanta Braves 7-2, and a rookie who had never thrown a single MLB pitch was the story.

Chad Dallas made his big league debut and did more than just hold his own. He allowed just one earned run in 3.2 innings, then combined with five Toronto relievers to hold Atlanta's powerful lineup to just four hits all game. Four hits. Against the Braves, who are 42-20 and leading the NL East by 9.5 games. That's a number worth sitting with for a moment.

On the other end was Chris Sale, who entered the night at 8-3 with full Cy Young Award conversation going around him. He gave up 10 hits in 5.2 innings and left with his record at 8-4. For a team that has been dominant all season, it was a rare off night from their ace.

Toronto's Myles Straw drove in three runs with two hits, giving the Blue Jays a comfortable cushion that the Braves' offense never threatened to close.

The good news for Atlanta? They're still 42-20. The best teams in baseball don't worry too much about one loss. They worry about how they come out the next day.

Who impressed you more — Dallas on debut or the Toronto pitching staff's combined performance?
facebook.image_caption: Blue Jays 7, Braves 2. Chad Dallas makes his MLB debut. Sale falls to 8-4. The Braves are still fine. ⚾ #BlueJays #Braves #MLB

article.headline: Blue Jays 7, Braves 2: Chad Dallas Debuts, Sale Stumbles as Toronto Hands Atlanta a Rare Beating

stories.title: Murakami's Historic Rookie HR Season — The White Sox Have a Star

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Munetaka Murakami is making history for the White Sox.

The rookie won AL Rookie of the Month for May and has been among the AL HR leaders all season.

Last year Chicago lost 121 games. Now they have a generational talent.

#WhiteSox #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Last year the White Sox lost 121 games.

This year Munetaka Murakami homered in 5 straight games (tied the White Sox franchise record), won AL Rookie of the Month, and is on pace to lead the AL in HRs.

Baseball is wild. #WhiteSox #MLB

facebook.long_form:
One year ago, the Chicago White Sox lost 121 games. That was not a misprint. One hundred and twenty-one losses — one of the worst seasons in modern baseball history.

Today they have Munetaka Murakami, and he is putting together one of the best rookie seasons the sport has seen in years.

Murakami won the AL Rookie of the Month award for May after hitting eight home runs in 26 games and slashing .244/.382/.556. He tied the White Sox franchise record with home runs in five consecutive games. Through the season so far, he has been among the AL's home run leaders — a statement that would have been almost unimaginable for this franchise just twelve months ago.

This is not just a baseball story. It is a story about how fast things can change in this sport. The White Sox committed to a full rebuild, absorbed the losses, and came out the other side with a player who makes every at-bat appointment viewing.

The AL Rookie of the Year race is still early, but Murakami has made his case impossible to ignore. The kid can hit.

What is the ceiling for Murakami this season? Drop your prediction below. ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: From 121 losses to an AL Rookie of the Month. Munetaka Murakami and the White Sox are building something. ⚾ #WhiteSox #MLB

article.headline: Murakami's Historic Rookie HR Season Makes the White Sox Turnaround Story Real

stories.title: Schwarber Is Chasing the 60-HR Club — And It's Not as Crazy as It Sounds

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber has 22 home runs. He's on pace for 66.

The 60-HR club has exactly three members: Bonds (73), McGwire (70, 65), Sosa (66, 64, 63).

Schwarber is in that conversation. In June.

#Phillies #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Let's talk about what Kyle Schwarber is doing — and what it would mean if he keeps doing it.

Schwarber has 22 home runs on the season. He hit No. 20 on May 15, the earliest date in Major League Baseball history that anyone has reached that milestone. He is currently on pace for 66 home runs over a full season.

Here is why that number matters: the 60-home run club has exactly three members. Barry Bonds hit 73 in 2001 and is the all-time single-season record holder. Mark McGwire hit 70 in 1998 and 65 in 1999. Sammy Sosa hit 66 in 1998, 64 in 2001, and 63 in 1999. That is it. No one else. Ever.

Schwarber is in that conversation right now. In early June. He is not just on a 50-HR pace — he is on the kind of pace that puts him in one of the most exclusive clubs in baseball history.

Obviously, sustaining this pace for five more months is incredibly difficult. But for right now, in June, what Schwarber is doing is genuinely unprecedented territory and worth paying attention to every single day.

Are you watching Schwarber's at-bats this season? You should be.
facebook.image_caption: Kyle Schwarber: 22 HRs, on pace for 66. The 60-HR club has exactly 3 members. He's in the conversation. ⚾ #Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Schwarber Is On Pace for 66 Home Runs — and the 60-HR Club Has Only Three Members

stories.title: AL East Race — Rays and Yankees Separated by One Game

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
AL East standings:

Tampa Bay: 36-22
New York Yankees: 36-25

One game separating first and second place. This division race is far from over.

#Rays #Yankees #MLB

facebook.long_form:
The AL East is the most-watched division in baseball. It almost always is. And right now, with the Tampa Bay Rays at 36-22 and the New York Yankees at 36-25, the race is as tight as it can get: one game separating first and second place.

Both teams have been remarkably consistent through the first third of the season. The Rays, without the luxury of the Yankees' payroll, have built a lean, effective roster that keeps finding ways to win. The Yankees, despite the roster changes and questions that followed last season, are back in the conversation and not going away.

Here is the reality for the rest of the league: the Blue Jays at 29-33 are fading from the conversation, and Baltimore and Boston are further back still. This is a two-team race, and it is going to produce premium baseball for the next four months.

Every head-to-head series between these two will be a referendum on who is actually better. The AL East always delivers in the second half. This year looks like no different.

Which team wins the AL East — Rays or Yankees?
facebook.image_caption: Tampa Bay 36-22. Yankees 36-25. One game apart. The AL East is heating up. ⚾ #Rays #Yankees #MLB

article.headline: AL East Tightens as Rays Hold a One-Game Lead Over Yankees — and the Race Is Just Starting

stories.title: Friday MLB Standings Check — Braves, Dodgers, Brewers All Dominating

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Friday standings check:

Atlanta Braves: 42-20 (best in NL)
LA Dodgers: 40-22 (leading NL West)
Milwaukee Brewers: 37-22 (leading NL Central)

Three dominant NL teams. October is going to be fun.

#MLB #Standings

facebook.long_form:
Three teams are separating themselves from the pack in the National League — and the picture is getting clearer every week.

The Atlanta Braves are 42-20 and lead the NL East by 9.5 games. The Los Angeles Dodgers are 40-22 and lead the NL West by 7 games over the Padres. The Milwaukee Brewers are 37-22 and lead the NL Central comfortably.

None of these division leads are safe in June. That is not how baseball works. But all three teams are playing at a level that suggests the 2026 NL postseason picture is already being drawn with these three at the center.

Atlanta, with the best record in baseball, has dominated all season despite injuries and lineup fluctuations. Los Angeles has that roster. Milwaukee's pitching staff is built to win in October. If the season ended today — it doesn't, but play along — the NL would send three very difficult opponents into the postseason.

Friday night games are in progress. Every win matters a little more now.

Which of these three is the NL's best team heading into the second half?
facebook.image_caption: Braves 42-20. Dodgers 40-22. Brewers 37-22. The NL standings are telling a story. ⚾ #MLB #Standings

article.headline: Three NL Teams Are Separating From the Pack — the 2026 Postseason Picture Is Taking Shape
